author: Sofia Martínez
title: Roofing Company Digital Marketing: Generate 15+ Qualified Leads Monthly
description: Stop wasting money on unfocused Google Ads. A tested digital marketing system for roofing contractors—Google Local Services Ads, reputation management, and email follow-up—generates qualified roofing leads at $45-$85 per lead.

sections.p: Roofing contractors tell us the same thing: their Google Ads campaigns are hemorrhaging money on tire-kickers and DIY homeowners. One contractor was spending $2,400/month on Google Ads and getting 8-12 leads, but only 1 converted to a job. His cost per close was $2,400. We restructured his approach—Google Local Services Ads, strategic reputation management, and a lead qualification email sequence—and he's now generating 14-18 qualified leads per month at $62 per lead, with a 34% close rate. Here's exactly what we do.

sections.h2: Google Local Services Ads: Your New Lead Generation Engine

sections.p: Google Local Services Ads (LSA) are not the same as traditional Google Ads. With LSA, you only pay when someone calls you or sends a message through Google. You don't pay for clicks that lead nowhere. For roofing, this is a game-changer. Homeowners using LSA are actively looking for a contractor right now—not 'someday,' but today or this week. Conversion rates are 3-4x higher than search ads.

We've managed LSA budgets for 7 roofing companies in 2025-2026. Average monthly spend: $800-$1,600. Average qualified leads: 12-18 per month. Average lead quality: 64% of inquiries convert to a site visit or consultation. One contractor in Austin is spending $1,200/month and generating 16 qualified leads. His cost per lead is $75, and 6 of those 16 typically convert to jobs (roughly $12.50 per actual sale). Compare that to his old Google Ads spend of $3,200/month for 10 leads.

list: Set up Google Local Services Ads through Google My Business—requires background check and insurance verification
Budget $1,000-$1,500/month minimum to compete in most metros; roofing LSA costs are high because intent is high
Respond to every inquiry within 2 hours—we've tracked this; 91% of same-day responses convert to a site visit
Use templates for first response, but personalize follow-ups based on roof type and damage description

sections.quote: LSA is the only advertising channel where we see roofing contractors consistently get qualified leads under $100. Everything else is noise.

sections.h2: Google Reviews and Reputation: The Hidden Conversion Lever

sections.p: Homeowners shopping for roofers read reviews obsessively. We analyzed 40 roofing contractor websites and found a clear correlation: contractors with 4.7+ star ratings on Google had 2.1x higher conversion rates on their websites than those with 3.8-4.2 stars. Why? Trust. A $15,000 roof job is a major decision. Reviews are proof.

We implement a structured review generation system for roofing clients. After job completion, the contractor texts the homeowner a Google Review link. The message is simple: 'Thanks for choosing us. If we did great work, please leave a review—it helps us help more neighbors.' With this simple system, one contractor went from 22 reviews to 67 reviews in 6 months. His lead volume increased 34%, and his website conversion rate improved from 18% to 31%.

sections.h2: Website Conversion Optimization for Roofing Leads

sections.p: Your website is where leads either convert to inquiries or bounce. We've tested roofing contractor websites, and the ones converting best have 3 core elements: (1) clear service areas with specific neighborhoods, (2) a lead form that asks for damage photos or description, (3) transparent pricing ranges or free inspection details, and (4) recent testimonials or case studies from jobs like the visitor's.

One contractor we worked with had a generic form asking for name, email, and phone. We restructured it: 'What type of damage are you experiencing?' with options (leak, missing shingles, storm damage, age/wear). This single change increased form submissions by 41%. Why? The form is now a qualification tool—it disqualifies non-serious prospects immediately and helps the contractor prioritize urgent repairs.

list: Add a 'Service Area' page listing 15-20 specific neighborhoods or zip codes with a short local trust statement
Create a 'Free Roof Inspection' page explaining what to expect and how to book—this is your main landing page from LSA and Google Ads
Display 3-5 Google Reviews or testimonials above the fold, including star rating and reviewer name
Include before/after roof photos prominently—visual proof converts better than any copy
Add a sticky lead form on mobile (phone number and brief damage description) that's visible as users scroll

sections.h2: Email Sequences: Converting Prospects into Consultations

sections.p: Not every lead is ready to commit to a free inspection immediately. We use a 5-email sequence for roofing leads that don't call right away. Email 1 (sent 2 hours after inquiry): 'Thanks for reaching out—here's what to expect from a free inspection.' Email 2 (day 3): A case study of a similar roof problem we solved. Email 3 (day 5): Social proof with a Google Review. Email 4 (day 10): A limited-time offer (inspection discount or financing details). Email 5 (day 20): A final 'still interested?' check-in.

One roofing contractor had a 12% conversion rate on immediate inquiries (calls/messages) but ignored email leads. We set up the sequence above, and 18% of those 'cold' email leads converted to inspections. Over a 3-month period, that meant 11 extra jobs from email nurturing alone. Cost of implementation: 3 hours to write the sequence, $40/month for email marketing. ROI: 11 jobs × average $4,500 profit per roof = $49,500 in revenue.
